decision: choose tokenization, Common Crawl/C4 funnel, open-corpus recipe board, deduplication, memorization/extraction, and DataComp-LM as first-wave data exhibits
rationale: These six surfaces cover mechanism, web supply chain, documented mixtures, repeated-data effects, leakage risk, and data-as-experiment without leaning on closed-corpus speculation.
blocked_claims: closed corpus composition; legality; exact shares; representativeness; quality rank
next_action: Use existing A-0064 through A-0067 where possible and add extraction rows before numeric redraws.

decision: hold FineWeb and RefinedWeb as reserves unless Chapter 17 needs modern web-processing texture beyond the existing funnel
rationale: They are valuable, but extra web-dataset pages can crowd the chapter unless they add a distinct processing or documentation job.
blocked_claims: best dataset; legal cleanliness; benchmark lift; web-data sufficiency
next_action: Capture metadata and page hashes before final use.

decision: use RedPajama and SlimPajama mainly as open-recipe/data-packaging reserves
rationale: They are strongest for showing open reconstruction work and dedup packaging, not proving model equivalence or impact.
blocked_claims: exact LLaMA replication; training equivalence; adoption; model performance; legal status

decision: defer ROOTS and BookCorpus to source-card or provenance-caution roles
rationale: These are evidence-rich but need careful excerpt and quote handling to avoid legal or governance overclaims.
blocked_claims: representative corpus; governance success; legal ruling; closed-model source share
next_action: Extract exact page snippets and quote limits before final card art.

decision: use long-context as a bridge exhibit only if it adds inference-time data pressure rather than repeating Gemini product visuals
rationale: Long context belongs to data because prompt/context supply becomes a second data channel, but it can easily drift into solved-memory claims.
blocked_claims: solved memory; solved retrieval; cheap processing; enterprise knowledge
next_action: Require model/version/date/context fields and cost/latency blockers.

decision: create a web dataset family tree or books/web/code triptych only if it replaces multiple smaller cards
rationale: Overview figures are helpful when they reduce clutter; they become filler if they merely add one more exhibit.
blocked_claims: completeness; quality hierarchy; legality; consent; closed-model mixture shares
next_action: Use as layout-pressure tools during final exhibit readiness.

decision: every data/corpus caption must name source type, date, transformation step, disclosed scope, and blocked closed-corpus/legal/exact-share claims
rationale: Data exhibits must show provenance and transformation, not ownership of truth about frontier corpora.
blocked_claims: closed-corpus composition; legal conclusion; representativeness; tokenizer economics; synthetic-data share
next_action: Carry blocker IDs into final captions and manifest rows.

decision: no new assets_manifest rows in this pass because no screenshot, raster, or final artwork was created
rationale: This pass ranks and governs candidates only; manifest updates belong to later capture/redraw execution.
blocked_claims: publication rights; raster hash; capture date; source-page integrity
next_action: Update assets_manifest.tsv only when new screenshots, source cards, or finished redraws exist.
